title: Creative Director Benevity · Canada (Remote) Actively recruiting
description: Meet Benevity Benevity is the way the world does good, providing companies (and their employees) with technology to take social action on the issues they care about. Through giving, volunteering, grantmaking, employee resource groups and micro-actions, we help most of the Fortune 100 brands build better cultures and use their power for good. We’re also one of the first B Corporations in Canada, meaning we’re as committed to purpose as we are to profits. We have people working all over the world, including Canada, Spain, Switzerland, the United Kingdom, the United States and more! High-Level Overview Are you a visionary creative ready to help shape the visual identity of a global brand? As our Creative Director, you will be the guardian of the Benevity brand, responsible for defining and evolving our brand’s visual and digital language to inspire action and drive social impact. You are equally at home concepting a campaign as you are building a project plan — a rare blend of creative and operational leader who thrives on both the big idea and the details that bring it to life. You will work at the intersection of strategy and execution, leading high-stakes creative projects and mentoring designers to elevate our profile as a category leader and the world's enterprise impact platform. This role is ideal for a leader who "grew up digital” and knows how to run a tight creative operation. Managing the processes, contributing to the work and mentoring designers to push their craft, leading high-stakes creative projects, and evolving our brand’s visual language to inspire action and drive global social impact. If you’re ready to take your creative career to the next level, we would love to hear from you! What You'll Do Creative Strategy: Develop and implement a comprehensive brand design strategy aligned with Benevity's business objectives to improve brand equity and visual consistency across all touchpoints. Visual Identity Leadership: Lead the evolution of Benevity’s visual identity system, across all media, ensuring it remains modern, accessible, and deeply resonant with our global audience. Creative Execution: Lead day to day execution and output of creative team and brainstorming for major marketing programs, product launches, and events. Develop a creative base for “big Ideas” that convert product and social impact data into emotional, thumb-stopping creative concepts. Direct, design and produce high-quality creative assets for major campaigns, product launches, and brand initiatives, ensuring excellence in typography, color, engagement (digital) and layout. Operational Leadership & Workload Management: Own the creative team's intake process, project prioritization, and capacity planning to ensure the right work gets done at the right time and that our teams can work at the speed of impact. Build and maintain systems that give the team clarity on priorities, deadlines, and bandwidth — balancing urgent requests against longer-horizon brand-building work without sacrificing quality. Collaboration across the business: Partner with Product, Marketing, Sales and People teams to ensure appropriate input and creative briefs that lend themselves to a unified brand experience across the entire customer and employee journey. Work with and influence stakeholders at all levels, including executives. Web oversight: Partner with the Digital Marketing team to deliver a high-performing, on-brand web experience, establishing and enforcing shared standards that drive improvements in performance, accessibility, and creative consistency. Mentorship & Influence: Provide creative direction and mentorship to the design team, supporting their design time while fostering a culture of innovation, excellence, experimentation and continuous learning. Team and Agency Management: In addition to managing internal team resources, oversee budget and external creative agencies and vendors to ensure timely project execution that meets Benevity’s high standards f
score_reason: Senior CD, remote, Canada-based, but lacking explicit USD/CAD compensation and unclear if scope matches Travis's integrated campaign + AI expertise at enterprise scale.

title: Creative Lead, Lifecycle & Organic Growth
description: This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for a Creative Lead, Lifecycle & Organic Growth in Canada. This is an exciting opportunity for a creative and strategic marketer who thrives at the intersection of storytelling, customer engagement, and organic growth. In this role, you will shape the voice and emotional connection of a fast-growing consumer platform used by millions of customers. You’ll lead the evolution of lifecycle communications across email, SMS, in-app messaging, and emerging organic channels, transforming transactional interactions into meaningful brand experiences. Working closely with product, growth, design, and customer insights teams, you’ll craft messaging that builds trust, drives retention, and strengthens long-term customer loyalty. This position offers a highly autonomous and collaborative environment where creativity, experimentation, and first-principles thinking are valued. It’s an ideal opportunity for someone who enjoys building from the ground up and making a visible impact on brand perception and customer growth. Accountabilities Lead the development and evolution of brand voice, tone, and messaging across lifecycle and organic growth channels. Create and optimize customer communications including email campaigns, SMS messaging, push notifications, in-app copy, onboarding flows, and retention programs. Collaborate with cross-functional teams including Product, Design, Growth, and Customer Insights to align messaging with user needs and business goals. Build customer trust and engagement through empathetic, human-centered storytelling and value-driven content. Analyze customer feedback, behavioral insights, and qualitative research to improve messaging effectiveness and customer experience. Develop and execute creative organic growth initiatives across channels such as social media, online communities, and content-driven engagement platforms. Contribute to customer retention, brand affinity, and long-term conversion through thoughtful and emotionally resonant communication strategies. Continuously experiment with messaging approaches, audience segmentation, and creative formats to optimize lifecycle performance. Requirements 5+ years of experience in creative marketing, lifecycle marketing, copywriting, or organic growth roles within consumer-focused environments. Strong expertise in writing compelling copy for email, SMS, in-app experiences, social media, and customer engagement campaigns. Proven ability to translate complex or technical topics into clear, engaging, and relatable messaging. Experience building or evolving brand voice and customer communication strategies in fast-paced or high-growth organizations. Deep understanding of audience engagement, community dynamics, and organic growth channels. Strong collaboration and communication skills with the ability to work effectively across product, marketing, and design teams. Analytical mindset with the ability to leverage qualitative insights and customer behavior data to inform creative decisions. Comfortable operating independently in a hands-on individual contributor role with significant ownership and autonomy. Bachelor’s degree or equivalent practical experience preferred. Portfolio demonstrating creative writing, lifecycle campaigns, and brand storytelling experience required. Benefits Competitive base salary ranging from CA$125,000 to CA$175,000 annually depending on experience, skills, and location. Equity opportunities as part of the total compensation package. Comprehensive health, dental, and vision insurance coverage. RRSP plan with employer matching contributions. Paid time off and paid parental leave programs. Wellness benefits and flexible remote work environment. Access to professional growth opportunities within a high-growth technology company. Collaborative and innovation-driven culture with significant ownership and impact. How Jobgether Works We use an AI-powered matc
score_reason: Senior creative scope in Canada (excellent), but lifecycle/DTC focus and lack of integrated campaign/brand leadership detail pulls it from 7+ to a solid 6—worth exploring but not a perfect fit for his integrated CD background.

title: Creative Strategist Superside · Canada (Remote) Actively recruiting
description: Superside is hiring a Creative Strategist to shape how we turn insights into breakthrough creative work. You’ll combine strategic depth with creative leadership, guiding a team of strategists, partnering with customers, and driving measurable business impact across brand, digital, social, and web experiences. In this role, you’ll set the standard for strategic excellence at Superside: mentoring your team, evolving our approach to insight-driven creativity, and helping some of the world’s most ambitious brands make work that matters. What You'll Do Oversee strategic development across multiple projects, ensuring clarity, quality, and creative consistency. Craft digital-first strategies for campaigns across social, web, display, newsletters, and emerging formats. Partner with Creative Leads, Designers, and Copywriters to turn insights into compelling creative concepts. Build trusted relationships with customers, uncovering challenges and turning them into strategic opportunities. Champion AI in the creative process, from research and insights to ideation, concept testing, and optimization. Identify and integrate AI tools and workflows that enhance the speed, quality, and scalability of strategy work. Leverage data and research to uncover audience, category, and competitive insights that fuel creative effectiveness. Collaborate with Sales and Account teams to expand strategic offerings and drive account growth. Contribute to the evolution of Superside’s Strategy discipline, defining best practices, frameworks, and future focus areas. What You'll Need To Succeed 6+ years in strategy roles within creative, advertising, or digital agencies Deep understanding of digital ecosystems, creative effectiveness, and platform storytelling. Proven ability to turn complex problems into clear, actionable strategies and creative briefs. Confident communicator and storyteller, skilled at presenting to senior clients and internal leaders. Data-informed thinker who blends analytical rigor with creative intuition. Organized and adaptable, able to manage multiple workstreams in a fast-paced, distributed environment. Entrepreneurial mindset: proactive, resourceful, and comfortable challenging convention to improve outcomes. Enthusiasm for innovation, emerging technologies, and the evolving intersection of AI, creativity, and strategy.
score_reason: Senior strategic leadership with AI integration at a creative services company, remote in Canada, but lacks traditional CD/ECD title and unclear compensation makes it an interesting stretch rather than a natural fit.

title: Creative Director (Graphics)
description: This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for a Creative Director (Graphics) in Canada. This role is a unique opportunity for a creative leader who thrives at the intersection of design, copywriting, and performance marketing. You will oversee the creative direction of email and SMS campaigns, ensuring every asset is visually compelling, strategically aligned, and optimized for engagement and conversion. Working closely with cross-functional teams and international clients, you will guide both graphics and copywriting teams while maintaining high standards of quality and brand consistency. The position combines strategic oversight with hands-on execution, making it ideal for someone who enjoys balancing leadership, creativity, and marketing performance. You will contribute directly to campaign revenue growth through high-converting customer communication strategies. The environment is fast-paced, collaborative, and outcome-driven, with a strong emphasis on innovation, accountability, and continuous improvement. This is a fully remote role offering flexibility while working with global brands and teams. Accountabilities In this role, you will lead the creative vision and execution of email and SMS marketing campaigns while managing and mentoring multidisciplinary creative teams. You will ensure that all creative output aligns with client goals, performance expectations, and brand standards. Oversee the quality and consistency of all copywriting and graphic design deliverables across email and SMS channels. Lead, mentor, and support the development of Copywriting and Graphics teams through coaching, feedback, and hiring support. Drive the creation of high-performing, conversion-focused campaigns that align with brand strategy and business goals. Collaborate with account management and strategy teams to align creative direction with client objectives and revenue targets. Review and optimize creative assets for mobile responsiveness and dark mode compatibility. Handle client feedback and escalations professionally while maintaining strong client relationships. Utilize Klaviyo and apply HTML/CSS knowledge to support email production and campaign execution. Ensure creative processes and outputs remain efficient, scalable, and performance-oriented. Requirements This role requires a strong creative leadership background combined with expertise in performance marketing, branding, and client communication within fast-paced eCommerce environments. 8+ years of professional experience, including at least 5 years in DTC eCommerce email, SMS, or CRM marketing. Strong expertise in branding, graphic design, and copywriting principles. Proven experience leading creative teams and managing high-quality campaign delivery. Hands-on experience with Klaviyo and familiarity with email marketing workflows. Proficiency in HTML and CSS for email design and development support. Experience working with international clients, particularly across North America and/or Australia. Strong communication and presentation skills across diverse stakeholders and time zones. Performance-driven mindset with the ability to balance creativity and business impact. Portfolio showcasing relevant creative work and campaign results. Willingness to participate in a paid assessment during the hiring process. Benefits Fully remote work environment with flexible collaboration. 30 days of paid time off annually. Paid public holidays. Internet allowance to support remote work setup. Health and wellness allowance. Maternity and paternity benefits. Loyalty rewards and recognition programs. Performance-based incentives and bonus opportunities. Outcome-focused culture that values creativity, accountability, and innovation. How Jobgether Works We use an AI-powered matching process to ensure your application is reviewed quickly, objectively, and fairly against the role's core requirements. Our system identifies the top-fitting candidat
score_reason: Senior remote creative role in Canada with integrated campaign scope, but email/SMS performance marketing is narrower than Travis's full-funnel integrated background (TV, OOH, packaging, large-scale campaigns).

title: Creative Strategist
description: TL;DR: We’ll know you’re the one if… - You’re equal parts performance-obsessed and creatively curious. - You don’t just want to make pretty things, you want to make creatives that convert. - You love digging into customer reviews, reddit posts, comments or anything posted about our brand. - You love digging through performance data to spot insights and translate them into clear direction. - You get excited when you see a great ad that’s converting. - You’re excited to test ideas fast and move on what’s working. If ads are launching weekly, performance is trending up, and the creative pipeline is moving without a hitch, you’re doing your job! The Creative Strategist Role You’re stepping into a high-growth direct-to-consumer company that offers you a whole lot of creative opportunities. As our Creative Strategist, you’ll help lead the charge in developing and launching high-performing ad creative. You’ll manage briefs and guide a lean-but-mighty team of editors and designers to bring ideas to life. This is a role for someone who loves the intersection of creative + performance, and thrives in fast-paced, test-and-learn environments. What You’ll Do: - Own creative ideation and strategy across brands, from concept through execution - Lead and manage briefs, ensuring clear expectations and strong alignment across teams Partner with Growth Marketing to interpret ad performance data and identify new creative angles to test - Manage timelines and deliverables across a team of editors, designers, and freelancers - Build and maintain a testing roadmap that prioritizes high-impact concepts - Source inspiration from top-performing competitors, platform trends, and cultural moments - Regularly report on creative performance and learnings, tying results to business goals - Help maintain a clear and organized creative tracker and testing log in Notion - Foster a culture of speed, experimentation, and ongoing improvement within the creative team You’ll love this role if… - You thrive in fast-paced, test-heavy creative environments - You get excited to look at data and use it to inform better creative - You love driving ideas from concept through delivery - You’re naturally curious and constantly collecting inspo - You can lead a team while still rolling up your sleeves You’ll hate this role if… - You want to make one creative and wait 3 months for feedback - You avoid performance metrics or creative critique - You prefer to just hand off a brief and be done - You struggle to self-motivate or keep the team on deadline - You typically avoid digging into the details Must-Have Requirements: - 2-3 years experience in a creative strategy, performance creative, or paid social role at a high-growth DTC brand or agency - Direct response copywriting and marketing knowledge, understanding and skills - Strong grasp of Meta ad creative best practices and how creative impacts ROAS/CAC - Experience managing and giving feedback to editors, designers, and other creatives - Comfort working cross-functionally across creative, growth, and brand teams - Proficiency in Notion, Slack, and basic asset organization/production tools - Solid understanding of performance metrics and ability to turn them into actionable insights - Strong sense of visual and narrative storytelling across digital platforms - Ability to balance big-picture strategy with day-to-day execution and delivery Life working for the brand: - Competitive Compensation – We offer a strong salary, tailored to attract top talent. - Performance Bonus – We offer attractive performance bonuses - Remote Work Environment – We are based in Australia but we can make the time difference work from wherever you are. - Autonomy & Ownership – You’ll have the freedom to run your own systems and make real impact - Growth & Development – We invest in your professional development with ongoing learning opportunities Why Join Us? Sondur Travel is a fast growing DTC brand with a small, nimble team and big goals. You will

title: Creative Director
description: Here at Hometap, we’re collaborative, passionate, and always ready to roll up our sleeves to create solutions that help people get more out of homeownership — and out of life. Our first product, a home equity investment, gives homeowners the opportunity to quickly access the equity they’ve built in their home to pay for what’s most important to them, from handling debt to finally making much-needed renovations, without taking on debt. We work hard, and have some fun along the way, too. You don’t have to take our word for it: The Boston Globe and Forbes have recognized us as a great place to work, and we’ve been honored for our commitment to innovation in the fintech space by Inc., HousingWire, and more! In case you need any more convincing, we also provide competitive compensation, healthcare coverage, generous PTO, a meaningful equity package, and a strong culture with plenty of opportunities for team-building and camaraderie. Interested in joining us on our mission to make homeownership less stressful and more accessible? We’d love to talk. Who You Are As Creative Director, you'll be responsible for defining and upholding the overall creative vision quality bar for creative production at Hometap. You'll ensure that all creative work — across campaigns, channels, and touchpoints — is cohesive, on-brand, and aligned to business goals. You'll lead the creative team, guiding creative contributors while overseeing creative production, resourcing, and external partners. You bring a strong point of view on storytelling, craft, and performance, ensuring that creative work not only looks and feels exceptional, but drives results. The Creative Director will partner closely with the Head of Brand Marketing to translate brand strategy into compelling, scalable creative expression. This role is responsible for elevating the work, improving how the team operates, and ensuring the creative function is set up to meet both current and future business needs. What You'll Do Own the overall creative vision and quality bar for creative production across workstreams ensuring quality, efficiency, and scalability Translate our brand vision into clear creative direction for our in-house and external creative partners/resources, providing input to their work and ensuring that their results meet the team needs for future iterations and applications Lead, mentor, and develop creative team members across disciplines Elevate the team’s ability to think conceptually, execute systematically, and work cross-functionally to meet project and business objectives Establish and refine processes that improve speed, output, and creative effectiveness Identify, evaluate, and manage external creative production vendors and partners Partner with Growth, Product Marketing, Content, and other teams to align creative with business goals in mind Continually update best practices for Hometap to drive response and action from our creative efforts; incorporate up-to-date research, channel best practices, and third party expertise (e.g. Google, Facebook, Media agency guidelines) to inform all our efforts Partner with creative project management to resource and prioritize work according to priority Bring forward-thinking ideas rooted in industry trends, performance insights, and cultural relevance What You’ll Bring 10+ years in a creative role, with 5+ years leading creative teams including copywriters, designers, and motions graphic artists Video and photography production experience Highly collaborative — you like to partner with and support the needs of a range of internal partners Experience mentoring a high-performance team; a people leader who can identify, recruit, and develop great talent to create premium work Comfortable sourcing, vetting, and managing external creative agencies, contractors, and freelancers. Creatively savvy; you stay current and ahead of the latest design, video, and other creative trends and digital advancements. Experience with and appreciation
score_reason: Senior CD title fits, but fintech/homeownership positioning is outside Travis's integrated campaign/brand DNA (Toyota, TELUS, Nissan, Diageo), and no mention of remote work or location flexibility is a major red flag for Costa Rica move.

title: Creative Director
description: For decades, DTN has been the silent force behind some of the world’s most critical industries—helping businesses navigate complexity, uncertainty, and risk with smarter, faster decisions. From agriculture to energy to weather intelligence, our proprietary Operational Decisioning Platform transforms raw data into decision-grade insights—enabling companies to optimize supply chains, ensure market stability, and safeguard infrastructure against disruption. We don’t follow trends—we set the standard for precision, trust, and operational impact. DTN is at an exciting inflection point. Building off a foundation of financial strength, profitability, and industry trust, we’re accelerating growth and expanding our global footprint. Our purpose-built solutions—powered by AI and honed by decades of vertical expertise—are helping some of the world’s most significant enterprises thrive amid operational constraints and uncover new opportunities in a fast-changing world. J ob Description: We are currently seeking a supremely skilled Creative Director to spearhead and mentor the DTN internal Creative Team while elevating DTN creative across all customer touchpoints. You will oversee the development and execution of every creative strategy, program and asset - including design, video, copywriting, website design and development while supporting three distinct business units and a diverse portfolio of solutions. You will report to the Chief Marketing Officer and manage a cross-disciplinary team of designers, writers, video producers, and developers, as well as select agency partners. Key Responsibilities Lead the In-House Creative Team: Recruit, mentor, and develop a team of designers, writers, and video/content creators, fostering a culture of innovation, collaboration, and high performance. Develop best-in-class creative for a variety of touchpoints - including but not limited to - design, brand, web, social, events, product, interface and more. Help lead and manage both in-house and external teams in creative strategy, execution and production. Creative Strategy & Vision: Own and drive the overall creative vision that aligns with company and business unit strategies, ensuring all campaigns are on-brand and on-message. Ai/Creative integration: Be comfortable with and able to work across several different Ai platforms from a creative and workflow perspective. Campaign Development: Develop and execute integrated creative campaigns for product launches, demand generation, digital marketing, sales enablement, events, and brand awareness across channels (web, social, email, video, print, etc.). Brand Consistency: Safeguard and evolve our brand standards, ensuring consistent messaging, tone, and visual identity across all touchpoints. Creative Management: Oversee creative project workflows, from initial concept through execution and delivery. Comfortable managing budgets and resources for maximum impact. Collaborative Partner: Work closely with marketing, sales, product, and executive teams to translate business objectives into compelling creative strategies. Data-Driven Approach: Leverage creative performance data and market insights to continuously improve effectiveness and drive ROI for marketing assets. Vendor Management: Oversee external creative vendors and agencies, ensuring alignment with internal capabilities and brand guidelines. Process Optimization: Implement scalable creative processes and best practices to improve quality, speed, and collaboration across the organization. Foster a collaborative and inclusive approach to your work, enabling cross team working to deliver the very best results Qualifications 10+ years of creative leadership experience in a B2B software, SaaS, or technology environment. Brings clarity to complexity through the power of storytelling - able to communicate intricate ideas with simplicity and impact. Proven track record overseeing high-performing creative teams delivering design, video, copywriting, and web

title: Creative Director, Event Design
description: Let’s cut to it. We’re after the kind of Creative Director who isn’t just “creative,” but creative in a way that’s wired straight into the zeitgeist. Someone who knows the difference between a good idea and a culture-shifting one, and who knows how to get it made. You’re a strategist’s strategist, a leader’s leader, and the person people trust to turn chaos into clarity. You’ve helmed experience end-to-end, built teams that punch above their weight, and still manage to make the room lighter and brighter when you walk in. You’re the benchmark others measure against. Why GMR? Because we’re not like the rest, and it’s not just something we say in pitch decks. We’re ingenious, flat by design, and allergic to big egos (ours or yours). We believe in ruthless transparency and radical support. We root for each other. We fight for the greatest ideas because it’s what’s right. We trade credit for collaboration and status for momentum, and right now, we’ve got more of that than ever. If you read this and think yes to all of this, it's time to talk. You might just do your best work here, and we’re set to let you fly. Please submit your portfolio or samples of your work to be considered for this role. ABOUT GMR MARKETING We are the Experience Agency Making Unforgettable Stories Born of Humanity NOT JUST EXPERIENTIAL. EXPERIENCE. The lines have blurred. People live in the physical, digital, and social worlds all at once, and your brand experiences should do the same. NOT JUST TELLING. MAKING. The best stories are lived, not heard. Make an immersive world for people to explore so they can experience something they’ll never forget. NOT JUST CONSUMERS. HUMANS. It all starts with the human. If you want to make a lasting impact, look beyond the wallet to understand what motivates and inspires people. REQUIRED SKILLS Lead creative that disrupts, not decorates. You’ll shape ideas from a blank page to award-winning, business-shifting campaigns. If it doesn’t make people talk, it’s not done yet. Be the insight hunter. You turn data, culture, and human behavior into creative gold by partnering with strategists to find the signal in the noise. Pitch like a legend. You own the room, turning concepts into must-buys and clients into believers, first meeting or a final sell. Accelerate from brief to brilliance. Your speed isn’t just about moving fast; it’s about moving smart, transforming briefs into work that sets new standards. Steer diverse creative experiences. Digital, social, experiential, emerging tech are in your vernacular. You lead teams and projects across every channel, every platform, every audience. Build strategic client relationships. You’re a problem-solver, a trusted advisor, and a creative diplomat that is always building and defending the kind of partnerships that make great work possible. Architect the team. You design team structures that balance talent, ambition, and sanity. You make sure the right people are in the right roles for maximum impact. Set the bar and raise it. You inspire, motivate, and demand excellence, creating a culture where great work is the baseline, not the exception. Give feedback that actually helps. You’re clear, direct, and real-time with your feedback which grows talent, not just critiquing it. Create a space for brilliance. Collaboration isn’t just a buzzword here at GMR. It’s how you build the environment where creativity thrives. Coach, mentor, elevate. You watch, encourage, and push your team to be better every day, ensuring consistency, growth, and a bit of magic in every project. Never stop learning. Curiosity isn’t optional. You’re relentlessly expanding your skills, your vision, and your impact. INCLUSION, BELONGING & IMPACT Our workplace is for ALL. We foster a culture where every background and perspective is valued so we can create meaningful memories that resonate with everyone. We aim to cultivate belonging by meeting people where they are, removing obstacles, and building on their strengths to
score_reason: Senior creative leadership at an experience agency with strong cultural fit, but no mention of remote work, salary, or location flexibility—critical unknowns for Travis's Costa Rica move.

title: Freelance Creative Director, Live Event & Experiential
description: Join a dynamic team as a Freelance Creative Director, where you'll lead the creative vision for a major tech event across wayfinding, scenic & spatial design, keynotes, and graphics. This role offers the opportunity to shape immersive experiences and collaborate with top-tier professionals in the industry. Oversee and refine scenic and environmental designs, collaborating closely with 3D designers and production teams for seamless execution. Guide creative direction across executive summits, including lounge and check-in experiences, environmental branding, and overall atmosphere. Direct the development of content and brand moments, ensuring cohesive integration across spaces. Provide creative direction for keynote motion and graphics packages, including theme loops, transitions, and stage visuals. Skills Proven experience in experiential design and live events, with a focus on shaping attendee journeys and physical environments. Strong expertise in motion and keynote presentations, with the ability to work across spatial, graphic, and content systems. Hands-on and collaborative approach, capable of stepping into ongoing projects and contributing effectively. Experience working with enterprise or tech clients, enhancing the ability to deliver tailored creative solutions. At Horizontal, we are committed to fostering a diverse, equitable, and inclusive environment where all individuals are valued and empowered to succeed. By applying for this position, you acknowledge and agree that Horizontal Talent may contact you regarding your application using automated technology, including phone calls, SMS/text messages, or email, which may be delivered by our virtual AI recruiter, Alex. Please apply through this online posting or by visiting our Job Board at www.horizontaltalent.com/job-board. Applications will be accepted for 4 weeks. For those that join the team, we offer competitive compensation and benefits including medical, dental, vision, and retirement. Check out all we have to offer and how you can become part of the Horizontal Talent Team. The pay range for this role is $75 - $80 per hour based on qualifications and experience.
score_reason: Senior experiential creative leadership with strong scope, but freelance structure, no compensation listed, and unclear remote policy limit fit.

title: Associate Creative Director - Healthcare
description: Piper Companies is seeking an Associate Creative Director – Healthcare to lead and elevate creative work for pharmaceutical and healthcare brands across DTC and HCP audiences. This role is a hands-on creative leadership position requiring strong conceptual thinking, executional excellence, and deep experience within regulated categories. This is a remote contract opportunity with preference of someone sitting in New York. Responsibilities for the Associate Creative Director – Healthcare include: Leading the ideation and development of campaign platforms, creative concepts, and visual systems across channels including social, display, email, web/landing pages, video, and print. Operating with a high degree of independence while collaborating closely with copywriters, UX designers, strategists, account leaders, and production partners to deliver integrated work. Actively contributing to day-to-day design and execution, ensuring all creative is polished, on-strategy, and exceptionally sound. Presenting creative work to internal teams and clients with confidence, clearly articulating conceptual rationale and guiding feedback and revisions. Navigating pharmaceutical MLR/Legal/Regulatory review processes, ensuring fair balance, safety language, and appropriate use of approved claims and indications. Leveraging AI and emerging creative technologies to accelerate ideation, moodboarding, visual exploration, layout iteration, and production workflows while maintaining brand safety and regulatory sensitivity. Qualifications for the Associate Creative Director – Healthcare include: A portfolio demonstrating strong conceptual thinking, visual storytelling, and integrated campaign development across digital and print channels. Proven experience leading creative work within pharmaceutical, healthcare, or other highly regulated categories. Strong digital fluency, including experience with web ecosystems, UX fundamentals, and conversion-focused design. Demonstrated ability to thrive in fast-paced environments while managing multiple stakeholders and deadlines. Expertise in Adobe Creative Suite (Photoshop, Illustrator, InDesign); After Effects and Premiere are a plus. Hands-on experience with Figma and Sketch for digital and product design workflows. Familiarity with modern AI-powered creative tools, with strong judgment around consent, usage rights, representation, and medical/pharma appropriateness. Compensation for the Associate Creative Director – Healthcare includes: Salary range: $150,000 - $165,000 Comprehensive benefits package including Medical, Dental, Vision, 401k, PTO, holidays, and sick leave as required by law. Keywords: Associate Creative Director, healthcare advertising, pharmaceutical marketing, creative leadership, conceptual development, integrated campaigns, visual storytelling, brand strategy, DTC marketing, HCP marketing, regulated categories, MLR and regulatory review, fair balance and safety, approved claims and indications, patient-centric communications, omnichannel campaigns, digital and print creative, social media, web and landing pages, video creative, digital design, UX fundamentals, cross-functional collaboration, client presentations, Adobe Creative Suite, Photoshop, Illustrator, InDesign, Figma, AI-assisted creative, creative ideation, layout iteration, brand safety, independent thinking, fast-paced environments #REMOTE This job is open for applications on 5/15/2026 and will remain open for at least 30 days from the posting date

title: Chief Marketing Officer
description: About Us We are a staffing services technology company that helps organizations design, build, and scale digital products and engineering capabilities. Our teams deliver end-to-end software development, engineering, and design services, and we provide flexible staffing solutions to augment internal teams with specialized talent—quickly and reliably. The Role We are seeking a visionary, results-driven Chief Marketing Officer (CMO) to join our executive leadership team. You’ll be responsible for owning the brand story, driving customer acquisition, and leading our global marketing strategy. You’ll collaborate with Sales, Product, and the CEO to align marketing initiatives with revenue targets, ensuring a cohesive customer journey from brand awareness to advocacy. What You’ll Do Brand Strategy & Positioning: Define and execute the company’s long-term brand vision; oversee messaging, visual identity, and market positioning to ensure our value proposition resonates with target audiences and differentiates us from competitors. Demand Generation & Growth: Lead the strategy for inbound and outbound marketing channels; oversee performance marketing, SEO/SEM, content marketing, and events to drive high-quality leads and measurable revenue growth. Product Marketing & Go-to-Market: Orchestrate successful product launches; work with Product teams to translate technical features into compelling benefits and ensure sales teams are equipped with the right enablement tools. Customer Lifecycle & Retention: Develop strategies to increase customer lifetime value (CLV); oversee customer engagement programs, loyalty initiatives, and communications that foster retention and reduce churn. Collaboration: Work closely with the Chief Revenue Officer (CRO) and Sales leadership to align on lead definitions, pipeline targets, and conversion strategies; partner with Product to infuse customer feedback into the roadmap. Administrative & Reporting: Manage the marketing budget and resource allocation; establish a data-driven culture by tracking KPIs (CAC, LTV, ROAS, MQL/SQL conversion) and presenting performance reports to the Board of Directors. Market Intelligence & Innovation: Monitor industry trends, competitive landscapes, and consumer behavior; identify new market opportunities and leverage emerging technologies (e.g., AI in marketing) to maintain a competitive edge. What We’re Looking For Experience: 10+ years of progressive marketing leadership experience, with at least 3-5 years in a VP or C-level role; experience scaling B2B SaaS or high-growth consumer brands is highly preferred. Education: Bachelor’s degree in Marketing, Business, or a related field; MBA is a strong plus. Communication: Exceptional storytelling and public speaking skills; ability to inspire internal teams and represent the brand externally to media, analysts, and investors. Leadership: Proven ability to build, mentor, and lead a diverse marketing organization across multiple disciplines (Creative, Demand Gen, Comms, Product Marketing). Strategic Acumen: Demonstrated success in connecting marketing activities to the bottom line; ability to balance brand-building with short-term performance targets. Problem Solving: Strong analytical mindset with the ability to interpret complex data sets to optimize spend and pivot strategies based on market feedback. Systems & Tools: Proficiency with the modern marketing stack (e.g., HubSpot, Salesforce, Marketo, Google Analytics) and familiarity with attribution modeling. Resilience & Adaptability: Strategic thinker who is creative yet pragmatic; comfortable navigating rapid growth and changing market dynamics. Team Collaboration: Collaborative executive presence; comfortable breaking down silos between Sales, Product, and Marketing to drive unified business goals Compensation & Benefit We believe in paying top-of-market rates for top-tier talent. The base salary range for this role is $275,000 to $325,000, with exact placement determined by your

title: VP, Marketing & Growth
description: Who We Are Goodway Group is one of AdAge's 2025's BEST PLACES TO WORK ! As an independent and remote-first media and marketing services firm with a 90+ year history, Goodway Group has the security of an established company combined with a start-up feel. With leading data-driven and technology-enabled digital media and marketing services firm with teams in the U.S. and the UK, our diverse team of digital strategists, media practitioners, technologists and data scientists have won the most prestigious awards for innovative marketing technology, impactful work and inclusive remote-first places to work including being honored as a multiyear winner in Ad Age Best Places to Work, Ad Exchanger's Best Use of Technology by an Agency Award and two MarTech Breakthrough Awards and a certified service partner to The Trade Desk. Who You Are Goodway Group is looking for a strategic, commercially minded VP, Marketing & Growth to lead how we show up in market and how marketing contributes to growth. This person will own Goodway’s positioning, external narrative, reputational strategy, sales enablement, demand generation, and the evolution of the marketing operating model. This is not a traditional brand or communications role. It requires a senior leader who can shape the story, build the engine behind it, and translate marketing into proof, pipeline, and commercial impact. The role will partner closely with the COO, CEO, Business Development, and line-of-business leaders to create a more unified market presence for Goodway, while ensuring Overline continues to be marketed appropriately as a distinct consulting brand. This leader will also help modernize how marketing operates by introducing clearer systems, stronger prioritization, AI-enabled workflows, and scalable delivery models that may include external partners and offshore resources. The goal is to build a lean, high-performing marketing function that is credible in market, useful to the commercial teams, and designed to scale. What You Will Do Own and evolve Goodway Group’s positioning, messaging, and market narrative across brand, PR, thought leadership, events, and executive visibility. Build and lead a commercial marketing engine that strengthens sales enablement, improves pitch quality, and creates a repeatable stream of proof points, case studies, testimonials, and market-facing content. Partner closely with Business Development, leadership, and line-of-business teams to align marketing activity with growth priorities, pipeline needs, and a more unified “One Goodway” market presence. Lead demand generation and reputational marketing efforts that increase awareness, consideration, and qualified inbound opportunity across Goodway’s priority markets. Assess and evolve the marketing operating model, including team structure, workflows, tools, partner mix, and the use of AI and offshore production capabilities to improve speed, quality, and efficiency. Establish stronger marketing systems, operating rhythms, and prioritization, so the function is focused, measurable, and tied to business outcomes rather than reactive activity. What You Bring Significant senior marketing leadership experience in a B2B, agency, consulting, media, retail media, commerce, martech, or complex services business. Proven success in positioning, commercial storytelling, demand generation, and building marketing programs that support growth and revenue outcomes. Strong experience creating sales enablement and market-facing materials that are actually used by commercial teams. Deep understanding of how agency or services businesses operate, including the realities of people-led delivery models, utilization, and commercial support. Experience leading lean teams and using a mix of internal resources, agencies, freelancers, AI tools, and ideally offshore or distributed delivery models to scale output effectively. Strong executive presence, stakeholder management skills, and the ability to operate strategically while

We are bonded by our shared purpose – driving innovation that helps millions of learners improve their lives and achieve their dreams through education. Focusing on college and career readiness, Cengage School equips students with the skills and confidence to succeed beyond the classroom. Through exclusive access to National Geographic Learning, Big Ideas Learning, Gale, Thorndike Press and Cengage curriculum and technology, it engages learners in core subjects and career pathways—empowering educators and inspiring student success. Our culture values inclusion, engagement, and discovery Our business is driven by our strong culture, and we know that creating an inclusive workplace is absolutely essential to the success of our company and our learners, as well as our individual well-being. We recognize the value of diverse perspectives in everything we do, and strive to ensure employees of all levels and backgrounds feel empowered to voice their ideas and bring their authentic selves to work. We achieve these priorities through programs, benefits, and initiatives that are integrated into the fabric of how we work every day. To learn more, please see https://www.cengagegroup.com/about/inclusion-and-belonging/. The role synthesizes market, customer, and performance insights to shape strategy and partners cross‑functionally to deliver a cohesive, differentiated story across products, audiences, and channels. Optimize win rates and reduce objections through targeted campaigns, journey optimization, and sales enablement. Build attribution discipline and performance dashboards to ensure accountability and impact across programs. Engagement Marketing (Brand, Lifecycle & Thought Leadership) Enhance brand affinity and engagement through coordinated campaigns, content, thought leadership, and storytelling. Partner with internal teams to advance brand reach, relevance, and consistency. Engage media, industry influencers, and strategic partners to reinforce NGL and Cengage K‑12 narratives. title: Senior Director, Content & Audience Growth
description: Swooped is a Talent Platform (not a staffing agency). We are not the employer for this role and do not make hiring decisions. Clicking "Apply" takes you to Swooped to review the role and hiring company details. About the Opportunity The company creates technology to help restaurants and local businesses succeed in a digital world, helping business owners operate, increase sales, engage customers, and keep employees happy. The organization is seeking a Senior Director, Brand Content & Audience Growth to build and lead the restaurant industry's definitive owned media brand. This role will own data storytelling & editorial production, social and creator initiatives, and audience development. The objective is to transform proprietary data across a vast number of locations into the stories, benchmarks, and intellectual property that operators, press, and LLMs cite as the default source on how restaurants actually work. This position resides within Brand Marketing and oversees highly visible, high-leverage content and audience systems that directly impact brand authority, pipeline, and long-term category leadership. This is a builder role that requires a growth mindset. The individual will design and pilot experiments, implement them across every unpaid surface (search, LLM answers, social, creator, community, newsletters, and in-product rails) while owning adoption, audience, and outcomes. This role operates with autonomy, influences senior stakeholders, and translates data, experimentation, and learning velocity into clear editorial and commercial decisions. The ideal candidate brings a media operator's taste and a systems builder's rigor, aiming to build durable audience engines, not just publish content. The organization is making a multi-year investment to build the definitive owned media property for the restaurant industry — a personality-led, data-rich, audience-first brand operators genuinely seek out. This area represents a significant investment in brand over the next two years and is purpose-built to meet the LLM moment head-on. Key Responsibilities Build and own the owned media network end-to-end, from editorial point of view and voice through production, distribution, audience growth, and ongoing optimization. Build a social and culture-led programming engine that turns the organization's point of view, data, and talent into moments operators actually care about — owning brand social channels, cultural franchises, creator and influencer programs, and the operator-creator ecosystem as one integrated system that builds fandom and extends the media brand's voice. Architect the organization's point of view on the market in partnership with Brand Strategy and PR/Comms: the connective tissue between what is believed, what is published, and how the market (including LLMs) hears it. Design and run growth experiments (franchises, formats, channels, AI-powered workflows) to rapidly learn what drives audience, citation share, and pipeline, and scale what works. Create clear, data-driven roadmaps that deliver near-term authority and audience wins while building toward a scalable, long-term media and data storytelling engine in partnership with PR/Comms, who lead earned media activation and all press relationships for data programs. Lead flagship franchises through their full lifecycle, including data programs and newsletters. From scrappy early pilots to operationalized, always-on programs. Drive measurable outcomes, including share of LLM citations on a fixed high-value query set, citation velocity and backlinks per release, newsletter growth and open rate. Partner cross-functionally with Revenue Marketing, Web, Brand Strategy, PR/Comms, Product Marketing, Product, Data, and Investor Relations to ship integrated programs. Apply AI-first strategies to content production, distribution, LLM visibility, and audience intelligence, improving speed, consistency, and the quality of every asset shipped. Lead and grow a senior, integrated
score_reason: Senior content leadership with integrated growth scope fits Travis's background, but lack of salary transparency, remote status unclear, and B2B/SaaS focus (vs. his consumer brand DNA) create meaningful gaps.
